title: Warehouse Ventilation Rate
summary: Minimum ventilation rate for general warehouses per ASHRAE Standard 62.1.
evidence_quote: Table 6-1 of Standard 62.1 recommends 0.3 L/(s·m²) of ventilation as a design criterion for warehouse ventilation

title: Bus Terminal Ventilation Concern
summary: Primary concerns in enclosed bus loading areas are health and safety problems requiring proper ventilation.
evidence_quote: The primary concerns with enclosed bus loading areas are health and safety problems, which must be handled by proper ventilation

title: Underground Garage CO Monitoring
summary: All underground garages should have facilities for testing CO concentration or periodic checks.
evidence_quote: All underground garages should have facilities for testing the CO concentration or should have the garage checked periodically.

title: Perchloric Acid Hood Duct Material
summary: Ducts for perchloric acid exhaust systems should be constructed of 316L stainless steel or suitable nonmetallic material.
evidence_quote: Ducts should be constructed of a stainless steel material, with a chromium and nickel content not less than that of 316L stainless steel, or of a suitable nonmetallic material.

title: Perchloric Acid Hood Washdown
summary: Exhaust system for perchloric acid hoods must have internal water washdown and drainage system.
evidence_quote: the exhaust system must be equipped with an internal water washdown and drainage system

title: Fume Hood Bypass Air Prevention
summary: Fume hoods with vertically rising sash sections should include provisions to prevent bypass of ceiling plenum air.
evidence_quote: Fume hoods with vertically rising sash sections should include provisions around the sash to prevent the bypass of ceiling plenum air into the fume hood.

title: Collections Zone Zoning
summary: Spaces in similar environmental zones should be kept physically contiguous for efficient ducting and construction.
evidence_quote: spaces in similar environmental zones should be kept physically contiguous, with the occupied collections zone and the unoccupied storage environments adjacent to one another.

title: Loading Dock Pressurization
summary: Loading docks should ideally be positively pressurized to mitigate energy transfer and reduce condensation risk.
evidence_quote: Loading docks should ideally be positively pressurized, and shared walls with collections zones should be carefully designed to mitigate any potential energy transfer

title: Vapor Diffusion Direction
summary: During humidification, vapor is typically forced outward through the envelope, potentially limiting interior control and damaging exterior.
evidence_quote: During humidification, vapor is typically forced outward through the envelope, and may limit the possible control of the interior environment and damage the structure’s exterior.

title: Cultural Heritage Latent Capacity
summary: Adequate latent capacity is critical in cultural heritage applications; often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs.
evidence_quote: adequate latent capacity is often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs, resulting in environments that may be able to maintain temperature control, but commonly see relative humidity conditions rise beyond safe limits
